title: Eliminate a Segment?
event: Reyes Co.'s South segment reports a $10,000 loss: contribution margin of $35,000 less fixed costs of $45,000, of which only $30,000 is avoidable. Compute the income effect of eliminating the segment.

computationPaths.narration: Dropping the segment kills its contribution margin but escapes only the AVOIDABLE fixed costs — the rest gets reallocated to survivors.

memorize.body: Effect of dropping = avoidable fixed costs saved − contribution margin lost. Negative → keep the segment.
A 'losing' segment whose CM exceeds its avoidable costs is subsidizing the rest of the company — its reported loss is an allocation illusion.

questions.prompt: CM lost $35,000, avoidable fixed costs $30,000. Income effect of eliminating the segment = ?

distractors.feedback: You assumed the reported loss disappears. Unavoidable costs survive the segment — the company is 5,000 WORSE off.
Magnitude right, sign wrong — the lost 35,000 CM exceeds the 30,000 saved. Eliminating HURTS by 5,000.
